text: PhotoPass on-device ML pivot incomplete. Beta testers waiting. Shipping the old architecture means users will feel the seams. First impressions can't be repeated.
NDCG LLM POC works but sampling not implemented. Thread limit not added. Partial implementation creates technical debt. Better to complete than to partially deploy.
Or Karan's change didn't work. Gater disabled. Should have been more careful. Rushing changes creates cascading fixes. Slow is smooth, smooth is fast.
/now API built but returns wrong timestamps. Validation should precede deployment. Automation that gives wrong answers is worse than no automation.
Privacy-first architecture for PhotoPass was right call. But halfway there is worse than not started. Users deserve complete experience, not transitional compromise.
Cavalry workflow documentation needs to be complete before video demo. End-to-end workflow must be seamless. Incomplete demos waste the audience's trust.
Open Mosque RSVP system ready but response rate low. The system works technically. The problem isn't the form, it's upstream strategy. Can't engineer around relational gaps.
PhotoPass pivoted from 30 to 10 challenges. Faster execution, yes. But are 10 challenges enough to prove value. Reducing scope risks shipping something too small to matter.
Every three hours notification system designed. Implementation pending. The architecture is sound but without the notification delivery mechanism, it's incomplete infrastructure.
iMessage integration most urgent. Not started because database access method unclear. Can't build on uncertain foundation. Research phase cannot be skipped.
MAPS Ready volunteer database needed. Google forms being prepared. But what happens when volume scales. Building intake without retention architecture creates future rework.
